scope: FQ-09A: give future human and LLM art passes a concrete asset map covering live assets and planned near-term content, with prompt packs specific enough that another model can produce candidates without repo archaeology. No art produced; no code/data changes.
summary: docs/ASSET_ROADMAP.md is the single asset authority (the queue's optional machine manifest was deliberately not created - nothing consumes one; the runtime resolves all art by convention, so the roadmap IS the manifest). It records: how the pipeline works for any art agent (drop a PNG at art/generated/<category>/<id>.png and it is live next entry; fallbacks always cover missing files; <id>_01..._08 variant pools give deterministic block variety and 8 fps opening cel frames; the validator treats gaps as INFO; text baked into any image is auto-rejected); live tables for every renderable id with path, size, transparency rule, current fallback, P1-P3 priority, and a per-id prompt note (11 blocks, 16 item icons, 3 live enemies, 9 equipment icons, dirt/stone back walls, 3 scenic backdrop layers, 8 opening scenes); the three drawn-shape actors that need a small renderer extension shipped alongside their first sprites (player - currently appearance-colored rects, town hall, attunement pulse); honest planned tables keyed to gating queue items (FQ-10 copper/iron/coal/tin/silver/crystal/rare ores, FQ-11 workbench/furnace/anvil + ingots, FQ-12 seeds/crops/tilled soil, FQ-13+ thornrat/ore_tick/raider_torchbearer then the data-planned roster and bosses, FQ-09M action effects, ore/fungal/crystal/timber walls and cave/deep backgrounds, phase C-E ancestry sprites incl. 6 dragonkin types, FQ-14/15 UI icons); and per-category prompt packs under one shared style preamble compressing the canon bible's palette roles, production rules, tone, and avoid list, with the template checklists as the review gate.
acceptance: Live and planned assets are separated (validator-locked headings; every planned family names its gating queue item and the doc forbids producing early)
Naming/prompt instructions are self-sufficient: paths, sizes, transparency rules, per-id notes, category packs, shared preamble, review gate
Planned coverage includes ores, stations, crops, enemies, tools/armor icons, UI icons, player/ancestry sprites, and action visuals
Opening, background, and back-wall entries restate their locked dimensions, transparency, fallback, layering, and no-baked-text rules
Validator green with the new required file and phrase locks; suite unchanged 210/210 (docs-only)
Haiku verifier fact-checked the inventory against blocks/items/enemies/equipment/ancestries data, prologue SCENES, and the wired backdrop/wall ids: NO FINDINGS
validation: python scripts/validate_repo.py -> PASS incl. asset roadmap authority
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed run -> 210/210 PASS at 2026-07-10T12:04:53
haiku read-only verifier -> NO FINDINGS (9/9 fact-check items)
decisions: No data/asset_manifest.json: the FQ-09A scope adds a machine manifest only if code/validation consumes one, and nothing does - avoiding a second source of truth that would drift from the convention-based runtime
art/generated/ui/ documented as reserved: no consumer reads it, so producing 32x32 icons now would create dead assets
Priorities encode operator-visible wins first: P1 terrain blocks + back walls (every screen), then player/hall renderer extensions; P2 loop surfaces; P3 presentation polish
Prompt packs compress the canon bible rather than quoting it, but defer to it explicitly - the roadmap cannot fork the style authority
Swarm: 2 parallel haiku inventory scouts (live ids + fallbacks; planned ids + citations), lead authorship, 1 haiku fact-check verifier
risks: Point-in-time inventory: future queue items must add their new ids to the roadmap or it drifts (stated in the doc)
Player/hall/pulse art is blocked on small renderer extensions; the roadmap requires shipping the code change with the sprites
The operator may reorder priorities at art-production time
next_action: FQ-09M: lightweight action animation pass. Art production can run in parallel using the roadmap's prompt packs.
